title: How to ACTUALLY Use AI For Coding
summary: Jack Roberts demonstrates Codex's /goal command, which initiates a loop called Ralph that autonomously plans, executes, and tests code steps without user intervention. Users describe the desired outcome in plain English and the system self-corrects if errors occur.
writeup: The video walks through Codex's /goal command, which starts an autonomous loop called Ralph that plans, executes, and tests code steps without intervention from the operator. Input is given as a plain-English description of the desired outcome, and the system self-corrects when errors occur. It matters because it shifts coding from manual step-by-step prompting toward specifying an end state and letting an agent loop iterate to reach it. The concrete takeaways: /goal triggers the Ralph loop, the loop handles planning, execution, and testing together, and the error-handling is built into the cycle rather than requiring manual fixes.
